resources.title: Python Basics
resources.summary: Start your programming journey with Python fundamentals and core concepts.
resources.resourceTip: Practice coding daily for 30 minutes to build muscle memory.
fullContent: Python installation and setup: Download from python.org and verify with 'python --version' command.
Variables and data types: Integers, floats, strings, booleans with simple examples.
Basic operators: Arithmetic (+, -, *, /), comparison (==, !=, <, >), and logical (and, or, not).
Input and output: Using print() for output and input() for taking user input.
Comments and code readability: Single-line (#) and multi-line (''') comments for documentation.
First program: Write 'Hello, World!' and basic calculator programs step-by-step.
nextSteps: Install Python on your computer and run your first 'Hello, World!' program.
Create 5 variables with different data types and print their values.
Write a simple calculator program for addition and subtraction.
Practice 10 basic exercises from online Python tutorials.
Read and understand error messages when code doesn't work.

resources.title: Python Data Structures
resources.summary: Master lists, tuples, dictionaries, and sets for organizing data effectively.
resources.resourceTip: Create real-life examples like shopping lists or contact books to practice.
fullContent: Lists: Creating, accessing, modifying, and common methods (append, remove, sort).
Tuples: Immutable sequences for fixed data with indexing and unpacking examples.
Dictionaries: Key-value pairs for structured data storage with practical use cases.
Sets: Unique collections for membership testing and mathematical operations.
String operations: Slicing, formatting, and common methods (split, join, replace).
List comprehensions: Write cleaner code with one-line list creation patterns.
nextSteps: Create a shopping list program using Python lists with add/remove features.
Build a simple contact book using dictionaries to store names and phone numbers.
Practice converting between different data structures (list to set, etc.).
Write 5 programs using list comprehensions for filtering and transforming data.
Solve 10 data structure exercises from practice platforms.

resources.title: Python Functions & Control Flow
resources.summary: Learn if-else conditions, loops, and functions to write powerful programs.
resources.resourceTip: Draw flowcharts before coding complex logic to visualize the program flow.
fullContent: Conditional statements: if, elif, else with real-world decision-making examples.
For loops: Iterate through lists, ranges, and dictionaries with practical use cases.
While loops: Repeat actions until conditions are met with break and continue.
Functions: Define reusable code blocks with parameters and return values.
Scope and arguments: Local vs global variables, default parameters, *args and **kwargs.
Error handling: Try-except blocks to handle errors gracefully without crashes.
nextSteps: Write a number guessing game using while loop and conditional statements.
Create 5 custom functions for common tasks like calculating average or finding max.
Build a simple menu-driven program using functions and loops.
Practice error handling by adding try-except to user input validation.
Solve 15 problems on control flow and functions from coding platforms.

guides.title: Python Practice Projects
guides.summary: Hands-on beginner projects to strengthen Python skills through building.
guides.resourceTip: Complete one project per week and document your learning journey.
fullContent: Project 1: Simple Calculator - Build a calculator with basic arithmetic operations and user menu.
Project 2: To-Do List App - Create a command-line task manager with add, view, and delete features.
Project 3: Number Guessing Game - Random number game with hints and score tracking.
Project 4: Password Generator - Generate secure random passwords with custom length and complexity.
Project 5: Basic Quiz Program - Multiple choice quiz with score calculation and result display.
Project 6: Contact Book - Store and manage contacts with search and update functionality.
Best practices: Code organization, meaningful variable names, and adding comments.
Next level: Add file storage to save data between program runs.
nextSteps: Choose Project 1 (Calculator) and complete it within 3 days.
Test your calculator with different inputs and fix any bugs you find.
Move to Project 2 (To-Do List) and implement all features step by step.
Share your completed projects with friends or online communities for feedback.
Complete all 6 projects within 6 weeks and build your own project idea.
